_note: Chapter 6 — Mona at Gerald's Baltimore clinic. Check in -> the truth -> surgery -> the mirror.

c6_van.name: The Van — Baltimore
c6_van.desc: No windows in the back, which says plenty. Prior drives like a man with an appointment he doesn't fear being late for, because the appointment is you and you're already in the van.

c6_reception.name: Clinic — Reception
c6_reception.desc: No sign outside, no magazines inside, a receptionist who never learns names as a professional courtesy. The consent forms are printed in a language of liability that isn't quite English. You sign somebody else's name. It's that kind of clinic.

c6_consult.name: Consulting Room
c6_consult.desc: Light like the inside of a refrigerator, a wall of before-and-afters with the eyes blacked out. Gerald studies your face the way Slick... the way an artist studies raw material: kindly, and entirely in terms of what it's about to become.

c6_room.name: Recovery Room — Before
c6_room.desc: A bed, a drip stand, a ceiling with forty-one tiles. On the side table somebody left a fan magazine: ANGIE — AMERICA'S DREAM, COMING EAST. She smiles up at the girl who is about to be spent like her loose change.

c6_theater.name: Surgical Theater
c6_theater.desc: The table takes you the way water takes a swimmer. Gerald's voice counts backward through a warm gray tide — and the last thing that is entirely Mona thinks, clear as Cleveland rain: remember your own face, girl. Somebody has to.
c6_theater.locked_text: Not until you've made the surgeon say out loud what he's been paid to do.

c6_mirror.name: Recovery Room — the Mirror
c6_mirror.desc: They saved the unveiling for day four, like a gift. In the glass: HER. The face from the magazine, the face from ten thousand screens, laid over your bones with terrible skill. You look for Mona and find her in the eyes — only the eyes. Hold onto the eyes.
c6_mirror.locked_text: There's nothing to see yet. There's barely a you.
